title: CDX Provenance Record
description: Schema for provenance/record.json in a CDX document

ancestors.description: Chain of ancestor hashes (nearest first; advisory cross-check, authoritative only where resolved — see 09 §3.3)

mergedFrom.description: Merged document hashes (each resolved like a parent — see 09 §3.4)

derivedFrom.description: Source documents this was derived from

timestamp.description: A trusted-timestamp anchor proving the document hash existed at or before a point in time (core 09 §6). Discriminated by `type` into three closed branches, each carrying exactly the fields its verification procedure requires. The timestamped hash MUST equal the record's `documentId` (and, on a frozen/published document, the signature-authenticated `manifest.id`) — see 09 §6.2; `time` is advisory only, the authoritative time comes from the validated proof (09 §6.6).

oneOf.description: RFC 3161 trusted-timestamp token. The token's messageImprint binds `hash`; a verifier validates the token's TSA certificate chain to a configured TSA trust store and confirms the imprint equals `hash` (09 §6.3).

time.description: Advisory claimed time (display only); the authoritative time is the validated token's genTime.

hash.description: The timestamped hash; MUST equal the record's documentId.

token.description: RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (a CMS SignedData), DER base64.

authority.description: Advisory TSA URL (display only); the TSA is authenticated by its certificate chain, not this field.

oneOf.description: Direct blockchain anchor: a transaction on `chain` in block `blockHash` commits `hash`. A verifier locates the transaction by `blockHash` (not height) on the canonical chain at the required confirmation/finality depth (09 §6.4).

time.description: Advisory claimed time (display only); the authoritative bound is the block's confirmation under chain-specific slack.

hash.description: The timestamped hash; MUST equal the record's documentId.

blockHeight.description: Block height (advisory locator; blockHash is authoritative under reorgs).

blockHash.description: Block hash — the reorg-resistant locator a verifier confirms is on the canonical chain.

merkleProof.description: Optional SPV proof of txId's inclusion in the block (a full node does not need it).

oneOf.description: Aggregated anchor (e.g. OpenTimestamps): many document hashes are batched into one `merkleRoot` committed on-chain by `anchor`. The Merkle leaf is `proof.documentHash` (which MUST equal the record's documentId and `hash`), tied to `merkleRoot` by `proof.path`. `provider`/`calendar` are advisory delivery hints, never trusted — the proof self-verifies against the on-chain anchor (09 §6.5).

time.description: Advisory claimed time (display only); the authoritative bound is the anchor block's confirmation.

hash.description: The timestamped hash; MUST equal both the record's documentId and proof.documentHash.

proof.description: Inclusion proof of documentHash in the aggregate Merkle tree.

documentHash.description: The Merkle leaf — the aggregated document hash; MUST equal the record's documentId.

path.description: Sibling chain from documentHash up to merkleRoot.

merkleRoot.description: The aggregate Merkle root committed on-chain by anchor.

calendar.description: Advisory calendar URL that delivers the proof; untrusted — the proof self-verifies against the on-chain anchor (display only).

position.description: Position of sibling in hash computation
